root_text.ud3.6:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.ud3.6:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā pilindavaccho bhikkhū vasalavādena samudācarati.
root_text.ud3.6:1.4: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud3.6:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud3.6:2.2: “ehi tvaṁ, bhikkhu, mama vacanena pilindavacchaṁ bhikkhuṁ āmantehi:
root_text.ud3.6:2.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho so bhikkhu bhagavato paṭissutvā yenāyasmā pilindavaccho tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ pilindavacchaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud3.6:3.1: “Evamāvuso”ti kho āyasmā pilindavaccho tassa bhikkhuno paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ pilindavacchaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.ud3.6:3.2: “saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, vaccha, bhikkhū vasalavādena samudācarasī”ti?
root_text.ud3.6:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmato pilindavacchassa pubbenivāsaṁ manasi karitvā bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.ud3.6:4.2: “mā kho tumhe, bhikkhave, vacchassa bhikkhuno ujjhāyittha.
root_text.ud3.6:4.3: Na, bhikkhave, vaccho dosantaro bhikkhū vasalavādena samudācarati.
root_text.ud3.6:4.4: Vacchassa, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pañca jātisatāni abbokiṇṇāni brāhmaṇakule paccājātāni.
root_text.ud3.6:5.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud3.6:6.4: So brāhmaṇo so samaṇo sa bhikkhū”ti.

translation_text.ud3.6:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.ud3.6:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Pilindavaccha addressed the mendicants as “lowlifes”.
translation_text.ud3.6:1.4: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.ud3.6:1.5: “Sir, Venerable Pilindavaccha addresses the mendicants as ‘lowlifes’.”
translation_text.ud3.6:2.1: So the Buddha addressed one of the monks,
translation_text.ud3.6:2.2: “Please, monk, in my name tell the mendicant Pilindavaccha that
translation_text.ud3.6:2.4: “Yes, sir,” that monk replied. He went to Pilindavaccha and said to him,
translation_text.ud3.6:3.1: “Yes, reverend,” Pilindavaccha replied. He went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.ud3.6:3.2: “Is it really true, Vaccha, that you addressed the mendicants as ‘lowlifes’?”
translation_text.ud3.6:4.1: Then, having applied his mind to Pilindavaccha’s past lives, the Buddha said to the mendicants,
translation_text.ud3.6:4.2: “Mendicants, don’t complain about the mendicant Vaccha.
translation_text.ud3.6:4.3: He doesn’t address the mendicants as ‘lowlifes’ out of hate.
translation_text.ud3.6:4.4: For five hundred lives without interruption he was reborn in a brahmin family.
translation_text.ud3.6:4.5: For a long time, he has addressed people as ‘lowlife’.
translation_text.ud3.6:4.6: That’s why he addresses the mendicants as ‘lowlifes’.”
translation_text.ud3.6:5.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud3.6:6.1: “In whom dwells no deceit or conceit,
translation_text.ud3.6:6.2: rid of greed, unselfish, with no need for hope,
translation_text.ud3.6:6.4: they are a brahmin, an ascetic, a mendicant.”

variant_text.ud3.6:4.5: samudāciṇṇo → ajjhāciṇṇo (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1, mr aṭṭhakathāyaṁ pāṭhantaraṁ)
